author: tersec
content:
I could try, but if possible I'd be just as happy to access whatever datastore might hold these, copy the logs, etc, and go from there. So far I've kept my LLM access set up with 0 credentials to anything in its own isolated space, so its blast radius is... well, I guess, the Internet. But nothing private

I've looked for old Docker logs before and I have no idea if they're even preserved

author: parithosh
content:
panda spins up a sandbox container to do anything, so your blast radius is kinda just the sandbox container. it can't access the host to do anything. it uses github oauth the to authenticate against the datasources. https://ethpandaops.io/posts/panda/ (sandbox section of the post tells you what permissions the sandbox is configured with)

You can also use panda as a standalone cli and use it to just return logs for you, but its gonna be a clunkier way to use it. 

but if you want to avoid the cli altogether, you can - the logs are visible in grafana and or via ssh + docker logs  😄

author: qu0b
content: Just ask the LLM for the right panda CLI commands and run them yourself 🙂

author: tersec
content: well, at any given time the docker logs cover 10-20 minutes as far as I can tell

author: tersec
content: unless there are more elsewhere that `docker logs` doesn't show. I already use that

author: parithosh
content:
that doesnt sound right, docker logs should have a much longer timeframe - its usually limimted by the size it takes up on the host and we used to set it at like 500mb or something relatively high.

if not, they will be visible in grafana under the explore tab, choose clickhouse logs as the option

author: tersec
content:
```
devops@nimbus-nethermind-1 ➜  ~ date && docker logs beacon | head -n1
Sat Jun  6 09:54:03 AM UTC 2026
WRN 2026-06-06 09:01:47.468+00:00 Unknown constants in config file           unknowns="[EIP7928_FORK_VERSION, MAX_REQUEST_PAYLOADS, MAX_REQUEST_INCLUSION_LIST, EIP7928_FORK_EPOCH, INCLUSION_LIST_DUE_BPS, MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST]"
devops@nimbus-nethermind-1 ➜  ~ docker logs beacon | wc             
 306389 4861067 65274070
```

author: qu0b
content: the amount of logs is pretty high 😅

author: qu0b
content:
```
panda clickhouse query clickhouse-raw "SELECT Timestamp, SeverityText, Body FROM external.otel_logs WHERE ServiceName='beacon' AND ResourceAttributes['network']='glamsterdam-devnet-5' AND ResourceAttributes['host.name']='nimbus-erigon-1' AND Timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR ORDER BY Timestamp DESC LIMIT 100"
```

author: qu0b
content: <@654267572107083777> you can switch out host.name `nimbus-erigon-1` with `nimbus-nethermind-1`

author: qu0b
content: and you should be able to get any interval of logs
